page: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with Hyper-Threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes a large 480 MB L3 cache, alongside 2 MB L2 and 48 KB L1d caches. It supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-threaded performance. Single-threaded gzip compression reaches a top-tier rate of 35.53 GB/sec, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 14.66 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations, such as bzip3 decompression at 102.1 GB/sec and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance at 16553.78, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 1210.65 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 132.44 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For machine learning workloads, the CPU's AMX instructions enable top-tier text generation speeds of 338.07 tokens/sec on small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to an average of 17.26 tokens/sec for medium 7B models.

A key architectural tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Premium Storage attachments. There are also no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D16s_v7 offers a balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads that require high single-core clock speeds and modern instruction sets without the overhead of dedicated local NVMe drives.
This server configuration is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving (benchmarked at 30.03 GB/sec), database operations, and lightweight CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C x86_64 processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs (8 physical cores with Hyper-Threading) running at 4.2 GHz, 64.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enabling top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.53 GB/sec and small-model LLM text generation at 338.07 tokens/sec. However, multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, requiring external Premium Storage for data persistence. This configuration is optimized for general-purpose workloads, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based inference tasks where a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose VM featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and AMX support, delivering top-tier single-threaded compression and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D16s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and AMX support, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and a classic one-to-four core-to-memory ratio.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation
No local storage included, requiring external Premium Storage attachments
Suited for general-purpose applications, database operations, and web serving
